title: pinus elliottii var. elliottii
intro: Pinus elliottii var. elliottii, also known as the southern yellow pine, is a subspecies of tree belonging to the pine family (Pinaceae) that is native to the southeastern United States. The resin of Pinus elliottii var. elliottii has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues. Some of the health benefits associated with the plant include its ability to reduce fever, soothe respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Pinus elliottii var. elliottii can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions like bronchitis, coughs, and skin irritations.
